metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5206
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2a, 3e: immunofluorescence imaging of hippocampal neurons stained for endogenous ELMO1 and PSD95 (DLG4) showed partial co-localization. Overexpressed GFP-ELMO1 localized to dendrites and spines apposed to presynaptic terminals labeled by anti-SV2.

Figure 2c-d, 3: confocal imaging showed that ELMO1 shRNA knockdown reduced dendritic spine density in hippocampal neurons and overexpression of the ELMO1 C-terminus promoted spine morphogenesis.

Figure 4: ELMO and Dock180 (DOCK1) form a complex in hippocampal neurons and function through activating Rac1.

Figure 5-7: RhoG is required for spine formation, its activation leads to increased spine head size and functions through the ELMO1/Dock180/Rac pathway.. Experimental description: M&M: "Neuronal Culture and Transfection - Hippocampal neuron cultures were prepared from embryonic day 19 rat embryos as described previously"

antibody:
rabbit polyclonal anti-Dock180 (1:1000, provided by Dr. Matsuda)
goat polyclonal anti-Dock180 antibodies (1:100, N19 and C19, Santa Cruz Biotechnology)
rabbit polyclonal anti-ELMO1 (1:1000, provided by Dr. Ravichandran)
goat polyclonal anti-ELMO1 (1:2000, Abcam)

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
